# infomaniak_public_cloud_user (Resource)

Manages an OpenStack user inside a Public Cloud project. Two creation modes are supported: direct (`invite = false`, `password` required) and invitation (`invite = true`, `email` required). After creation, `email`, `password` and `description` are independently PATCH-able. Sensitive fields are stored in state (the API never returns them, so drift detection on those values is not possible).

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "infomaniak_public_cloud_user" "ops" {
  public_cloud_id         = 1234
  public_cloud_project_id = 5678
  password                = "Sup3r-Secret!"
  description             = "operator account"
}

resource "infomaniak_public_cloud_user" "invited" {
  public_cloud_id         = 1234
  public_cloud_project_id = 5678
  invite                  = true
  email                   = "ops@example.test"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `public_cloud_id` (Number) Identifier of the parent Public Cloud product. Forces replacement on change.
- `public_cloud_project_id` (Number) Identifier of the parent project. Forces replacement on change.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Free-form description. The API rejects values containing characters outside `[A-Za-z0-9 -_.]` and longer than 250 characters.
- `email` (String, Sensitive) Email address. Required when `invite = true`. PATCH-able after creation.
- `invite` (Boolean) If `true`, the user is created via invitation email instead of direct password creation. Forces replacement on change.
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Password. Required when `invite = false`. PATCH-able after creation. Stored in state.

### Read-Only

- `created_at` (Number) Creation timestamp (Unix seconds).
- `id` (Number) Identifier of the user.
- `open_stack_name` (String) Underlying OpenStack user name.
- `status` (String) Lifecycle status of the user.
- `updated_at` (Number) Last update timestamp (Unix seconds).
